title: Meta Removes New AI Image Tool After Privacy Backlash
subtitle: The company faced strong criticism from users and labor unions over how it used public photos for artificial intelligence.

summary.en: Meta has removed its new AI image feature, Muse Image, shortly after its launch. The tool allowed users to create images using public Instagram content. Many people and groups criticized the company for turning the feature on automatically. A major actors' union called the move a dangerous mistake regarding user privacy. Meta eventually decided to stop the feature, admitting it did not meet user expectations.
summary.zh: Meta 在推出新的人工智慧影像功能「繆思影像」後不久，便將其移除。該工具原先允許使用者利用公開的 Instagram 內容來創作影像。由於該功能被預設為自動開啟，引發許多民眾與團體的批評。一個主要的演員工會稱此舉為危險的隱私錯誤。Meta 最終決定停止該功能，並承認其未能符合使用者的期待。

paragraphs.en: Meta, the company that owns Facebook and Instagram, has decided to stop a new artificial intelligence feature just days after it was released. The tool, called Muse Image, was designed to let users create and edit images using content from public Instagram accounts. However, the feature was quickly removed following widespread complaints about how the company handles user privacy.
paragraphs.zh: 擁有臉書（Facebook）與 Instagram 的母公司 Meta，在推出一項新的人工智慧功能僅僅幾天後，就決定將其喊停。這項名為「繆思影像」（Muse Image）的工具，原本設計目的是讓使用者能運用公開 Instagram 帳號的內容來創作與編輯影像。然而，由於大眾對於該公司處理使用者隱私的方式感到強烈不滿，這項功能隨即被移除。

paragraphs.en: The feature was part of the Meta AI chatbot and was developed by the company’s Meta Superintelligence Labs. When it launched on Tuesday, it allowed people to use photos as a starting point to create new images or change them using simple sketches. While Meta intended for this to be a creative tool, many users were unhappy that the feature was turned on automatically, a process known as an 'opt-in' system where users are included by default unless they choose to leave.
paragraphs.zh: 該功能隸屬於 Meta AI 聊天機器人的一部分，由該公司的「Meta 超級智慧實驗室」（Meta Superintelligence Labs）開發。當它於週二上線時，允許使用者以照片作為起點，透過簡單的草圖來創作新影像或進行修改。儘管 Meta 的初衷是提供一項創意工具，但許多使用者對於該功能被自動開啟感到不滿；這是一種預設加入的機制，意即除非使用者主動選擇退出，否則系統會預設將其納入。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian Technology, the backlash was immediate. Users were concerned that their personal photos could be used by others without clear permission. One of the most famous people to speak out was Emmy-winning actor Hannah Einbinder. She used her own Instagram account to warn her followers about the feature, telling them that it had been activated without their knowledge and encouraging them to turn it off.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報科技》（The Guardian Technology）報導，反彈聲浪隨即而至。使用者擔心自己的個人照片可能會在未經明確許可的情況下被他人使用。曾獲艾美獎（Emmy Award）肯定的演員漢娜·愛因賓德（Hannah Einbinder）是公開發聲的名人之一。她透過自己的 Instagram 帳號警告追蹤者關於此功能的風險，告知他們該功能在未經知情的情況下被啟動，並鼓勵大家將其關閉。

paragraphs.en: The criticism was not limited to individual users. SAG-AFTRA, a large union that represents actors and other media professionals, also took a strong stand. On Thursday, the union told its members and all Instagram users to opt out of the feature. In a statement reported by The Guardian Technology, the union argued that any use of a person’s images for AI must require a 'clear and conspicuous' choice, meaning users should have to actively agree to it before it happens. They described the company's approach as an 'unacceptable' mistake that ignored the public's fear of how their digital images might be used by others.
paragraphs.zh: 批評聲浪不僅限於個別使用者。代表演員及其他媒體專業人士的大型工會「美國演員工會暨美國廣播電視演員工會聯合會」（SAG-AFTRA）也採取了強硬立場。週四，該工會呼籲其成員及所有 Instagram 使用者退出此功能。根據《衛報科技》報導的聲明，工會主張任何將個人影像用於人工智慧的行為，都必須經過「清晰且顯著」的選擇程序，意即使用者必須在事前主動同意。他們形容該公司的做法是一個「無法接受」的錯誤，忽視了大眾對於數位影像可能遭他人濫用的恐懼。

paragraphs.en: Following this pressure, Meta released a statement explaining its decision to remove the tool. The company said that its goal was to provide a useful creative tool while giving people control over their content. However, they admitted that the feature 'missed the mark,' a common way of saying it failed to meet expectations or was not done correctly. Because of the negative feedback, the company decided to make it unavailable.
paragraphs.zh: 在面臨這些壓力後，Meta 發布聲明解釋了移除該工具的決定。公司表示，其目標是在提供實用創意工具的同時，讓使用者能掌控自己的內容。然而，他們承認該功能「未達預期」，這是一種常見的說法，意指該功能未能符合期待或執行不當。由於負面回饋不斷，公司決定將其下架。

paragraphs.en: After Meta announced the removal of Muse Image, SAG-AFTRA expressed support for the decision. A spokesperson for the union noted that the dangers of creating digital copies of people without their agreement are well known. They added that a feature encouraging such behavior was 'unwise' and that stopping it was the 'responsible thing to do.'
paragraphs.zh: 在 Meta 宣布移除「繆思影像」後，美國演員工會暨美國廣播電視演員工會聯合會對此決定表示支持。該工會發言人指出，在未經當事人同意下製作數位複製品的危險性是眾所周知的。他們補充說，鼓勵此類行為的功能是「不明智的」，而將其停止是「負責任的做法」。

paragraphs.en: This event highlights a growing conflict in the technology industry. As AI tools become more powerful, companies are under increasing pressure to be transparent about how they use public data. Many people are worried that their photos, videos, and writing are being used to train AI models without their consent. The quick reversal by Meta shows that technology companies are finding it harder to launch new features that rely on user content without giving people clear, easy ways to control their own information.
paragraphs.zh: 此事件凸顯了科技產業中日益增長的衝突。隨著人工智慧工具變得愈發強大，科技公司在如何使用公開數據方面面臨越來越大的透明度壓力。許多人擔心自己的照片、影片與文字在未經同意的情況下被用於訓練人工智慧模型。Meta 的快速轉向顯示，科技公司若無法提供清晰且簡易的方式讓使用者掌控個人資訊，將會越來越難以推出依賴使用者內容的新功能。

paragraphs.en: For now, the removal of Muse Image serves as a reminder of the power that users and organizations have when they speak out against privacy risks. As AI continues to change how we interact with social media, the debate over who owns and controls our digital identity will likely continue. Companies will need to find a better balance between innovation and the rights of the people who use their platforms every day.
paragraphs.zh: 目前，「繆思影像」的移除提醒了我們，當使用者與組織針對隱私風險發聲時，所具備的影響力有多大。隨著人工智慧持續改變我們與社群媒體互動的方式，關於誰擁有並掌控數位身分的爭論很可能會持續下去。科技公司將需要在創新與每日使用其平台的民眾權益之間，找到更好的平衡點。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To express your opinion publicly and strongly, especially about something you believe is wrong.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 公開發表意見（特別是針對不公義之事）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此片語由動詞 speak 與副詞 out 組成，意思並非字面上的「說出來」，而是指公開表達立場。文中：One of the most famous people to speak out was Emmy-winning actor Hannah Einbinder.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To express a firm and determined opinion about something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 此慣用語形容對某事採取堅定且明確的態度。文中：SAG-AFTRA, a large union that represents actors and other media professionals, also took a strong stand.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To fail to achieve the desired result or to fail to be accurate.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 未達預期、失敗、沒抓到重點。
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面意思為「沒射中靶心」，引申為未能達到標準或預期效果。文中：However, they admitted that the feature 'missed the mark,' a common way of saying it failed to meet expectations or was not done correctly.

sources.name: The Guardian Technology
sources.title: Meta ditches Muse Image AI feature because it ‘misses the mark’ on users’ privacy

quiz.question: What specific action did the union SAG-AFTRA take in response to the Muse Image feature?

options.A: They filed a formal lawsuit against Meta for copyright infringement.
options.B: They advised their members and Instagram users to opt out of the tool.
options.C: They demanded that Meta delete all images already stored in their database.
options.D: They created their own competing AI tool to demonstrate better privacy standards.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第四段，SAG-AFTRA 明確建議其成員及所有 Instagram 用戶採取「opt out」（退出）該功能的行動。選項 A 錯誤，文中並未提到訴訟；選項 C 錯誤，文中僅提到要求用戶選擇退出，而非要求刪除資料庫；選項 D 錯誤，文中並無提到工會開發自己的 AI 工具。

quiz.question: Based on the text, why was the 'opt-in' system described in the article a significant issue for users?

options.A: It required users to pay a subscription fee to access the Muse Image tool.
options.B: It prevented users from ever deleting their photos from Instagram.
options.C: It automatically enrolled users in a program they did not explicitly agree to.
options.D: It made it impossible for users to share their photos with friends.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第二段解釋了 'opt-in' 在此處的定義為「預設包含」，意即用戶在未經明確同意的情況下就被自動加入功能。這與第四段提到的「clear and conspicuous choice」（明確且顯眼的選擇）相對立。選項 A、B、D 皆未在文中提及，屬於無中生有的選項，無法由文中資訊推論得出。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the phrase 'missed the mark' mean in the context of Meta's statement?

options.A: Failed to achieve the intended goal or standard.
options.C: Hit a target audience that was too small.
options.D: Changed the original plan without informing users.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。文中提到 Meta 承認該功能 'missed the mark'，隨後解釋這代表該功能「未能達到預期」或「處理不當」。這是一個慣用語，意指目標未達成。選項 B 錯誤，文中未提到刪除數據；選項 C 錯誤，此處與受眾大小無關；選項 D 雖然有提到變更，但並非該片語的核心含義。

quiz.question: What is the central message regarding the conflict described in the article?

options.A: AI technology is too dangerous to be used by social media companies.
options.B: Celebrities are the only people who have enough influence to change tech policies.
options.C: Meta is the only company struggling with privacy issues in the AI industry.
options.D: There is a growing tension between AI innovation and the need for user control over data.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。文章第七段明確指出此事件凸顯了科技產業中日益增長的衝突：隨著 AI 工具變得強大，企業面臨更大的壓力，必須在創新與用戶數據隱私控制之間取得平衡。選項 A 過度推論；選項 B 錯誤，文中也提到了工會與一般用戶的影響力；選項 C 錯誤，文中提到這是整個科技產業的普遍現象，而非 Meta 單一公司。
